name: Doping & Point Defects
description: Doping a semiconductor introduces substitutional impurities that act as donors or acceptors: thermal ionization releases electrons or holes, charge neutrality is maintained through compensating defects described by Kroger-Vink notation, and the resulting carrier concentration sets the conductivity and type of the doped material.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Pure semiconductor"]
    N2["Introduce dopant atoms"]
    N3{"Donor or acceptor?"}
    N4["Donor (n-type): extra e-"]
    N5["Acceptor (p-type): holes"]
    N6["Ionize at temperature"]
    N7["Defect balance (Kroger-Vink)"]
    N8["Carrier concentration set"]
    N9["Conductivity tuned"]
    N10["Doped material"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|donor| N4
    N3 -->|acceptor| N5
    N4 --> N6
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
